description.source: Importing offline results can be done in bulk via excel
description.target: Importing offline results can be done in bulk via excel

body:0001.source: What is bulk importing via Excel?
body:0001.target: What is bulk importing via Excel?

body:0002.source: Sometimes you collect ideas or survey responses outside of Go Vocal, for example, during an offline workshop or through another tool. To make sure you can still use these results on the platform, we provide a way to import them in bulk using a fillable Excel template.
body:0002.target: Sometimes you collect ideas or survey responses outside of Go Vocal, for example, during an offline workshop or through another tool. To make sure you can still use these results on the platform, we provide a way to import them in bulk using a fillable Excel template.

body:0004.source: Bring external data into the platform.
body:0004.target: Bring external data into the platform.

body:0006.source: Take advantage of Go Vocal’s features (like analysis, tagging, or reporting).
body:0006.target: Take advantage of Go Vocal’s features (like analysis, tagging, or reporting).

body:0007.source: Because the upload process requires a strict format, this article will guide you through it in three steps:
body:0007.target: Because the upload process requires a strict format, this article will guide you through it in three steps:

body:0011.source: How to access the excel template?
body:0011.target: How to access the excel template?

body:0013.source: Go to the Input form tab of the phase → click Download an Excel Template.
body:0013.target: Go to the Input form tab of the phase → click Download an Excel Template.

body:0015.source: Go to the Survey Form tab → click the three-dot menu → select Download an Excel Template.
body:0015.target: Go to the Survey Form tab → click the three-dot menu → select Download an Excel Template.

body:0016.source: How to fill out the excel template?
body:0016.target: How to fill out the excel template?

body:0017.source: Example row: Row 2 shows how the template must be filled in. Follow it exactly — the system is case sensitive.
body:0017.target: Example row: Row 2 shows how the template must be filled in. Follow it exactly — the system is case sensitive.

body:0019.source: These columns are optional - if left blank the input will be imported anonymously
body:0019.target: These columns are optional - if left blank the input will be imported anonymously

body:0020.source: Add an “X” in the Permission column to upload personal details
body:0020.target: Add an “X” in the Permission column to upload personal details

body:0021.source: Remove any trailing spaces in email addresses
body:0021.target: Remove any trailing spaces in email addresses

body:0023.source: This column is optional - if left blank the system will add the date of import by default
body:0023.target: This column is optional - if left blank the system will add the date of import by default

body:0024.source: Use hyphens (YYYY-MM-DD) instead of slashes (/). You may need to set the cell to text format.
body:0024.target: Use hyphens (YYYY-MM-DD) instead of slashes (/). You may need to set the cell to text format.

body:0026.source: Coordinates: Must follow the exact format from the example.
body:0026.target: Coordinates: Must follow the exact format from the example.

body:0027.source: Multi-choice & Tags: Must exactly match platform wording (capitalization, spacing). Separate multiple entries with a semicolon ;.
body:0027.target: Multi-choice & Tags: Must exactly match platform wording (capitalization, spacing). Separate multiple entries with a semicolon ;.

body:0029.source: Image URL: Only one image per idea. Link must end with .jpeg, .jpg, or .png.
body:0029.target: Image URL: Only one image per idea. Link must end with .jpeg, .jpg, or .png.

body:0030.source: How to import the excel file?
body:0030.target: How to import the excel file?

body:0031.source: For Ideas: Go to the Input Manager tab → click Import → click Import again (top right).
body:0031.target: For Ideas: Go to the Input Manager tab → click Import → click Import again (top right).

body:0032.source: For Surveys: Go to the Insights tab → click Import → click Import again (top right).
body:0032.target: For Surveys: Go to the Insights tab → click Import → click Import again (top right).

body:0035.source: If your file contains personal data (name, email, etc.), check the consent box.
body:0035.target: If your file contains personal data (name, email, etc.), check the consent box.

body:0036.source: Imported entries will first appear as drafts in the Importer UI.
body:0036.target: Imported entries will first appear as drafts in the Importer UI.

body:0037.source: Once you Approve, they will be published into the phase and visible in results/input manager.
body:0037.target: Once you Approve, they will be published into the phase and visible in results/input manager.

body:0038.source: ℹ️ For ideation: approved imported entries are automatically tagged with “imported”.
body:0038.target: ℹ️ For ideation: approved imported entries are automatically tagged with “imported”.

body:0039.source: What are the limiations of bulk importing via excel?
body:0039.target: What are the limiations of bulk importing via excel?

body:0040.source: The following cannot be uploaded via Excel:
body:0040.target: The following cannot be uploaded via Excel:

body:0045.source: ⚠️ Do not add columns/questions manually to the Excel file. Always add them first in the online form, then re-download the updated template.
body:0045.target: ⚠️ Do not add columns/questions manually to the Excel file. Always add them first in the online form, then re-download the updated template.
